thread: Can the partial ritual data become a usable answer before more sites are lost?

evidence.clue: The farmhouse read proves the body is part of the plague lock rather than only a carrier.
Scholomance and Kel'Thuzad complete the resonance model and the waking sequence.
Chapter 13 translates the finished model into field language: the grain is the shell, the body learns the rest, and death lets it answer.

Chapter 13 proves the completed mechanism is immediately usable in field language when Jaina tells Sylvanas that the grain carries the shell, the body learns the rest, and death releases the prepared answer.

Chapter 14 turns the completed mechanism into countermeasure logic: granaries, bakehouses, and holding yards become the first concrete points where the outer shell might still be broken before it vanishes into bodies.
Antonidas now hears the broad limits and narrow promise of the mechanism, while Sylvanas carries the stripped operational version on the road to Stratholme.
Jaina turns the completed mechanism into a concrete city-side countermeasure plan built around stock before ingestion finishes the work.

readerKnows: The mechanism stays resolved. The live question is no longer how the plague works but whether its feed points can still be interrupted in time.
povCharacterKnows: Jaina knows the full model, Sylvanas knows the field version, and both now know what the first intervention targets inside Stratholme must be.
notes: Resolved at the theory level earlier; chapter 15 advances only the operational use of that answer.
